Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: 3-D printed ultrastrong heat-resistant aluminum alloy achieved by bioinspired nanoscale amorphous confinement

Composition_Info.Alloy_Name_Raw: FVS0812 aluminum alloy with 2 wt.% TiB2 nanoparticles
Composition_Info.Note: 2 wt.% TiB2 nanoparticles were incorporated into the FVS0812 powder as nucleants.

Process_Text.original: 3D printing via laser powder bed fusion (LPBF) was performed using an EP-M150 Metal 3D Printer with the FVS0812 powders incorporating 2 wt.% TiB2 nanoparticles. The process was conducted under an argon atmosphere with a laser power of 150 W, a scan speed of 1000 mm/s, a scan spacing of 0.08 mm, and a layer thickness of 0.02 mm. The build platform was preheated to 200°C during printing.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF in an Argon atmosphere. Preheated build plate at 200°C.

Microstructure_Text.original: The alloy primarily comprises a fusion zone (FZ) which constitutes the majority of the molten pool... A small amount (approximately 1%) of irregularly shaped precipitates, identified as the Al8Fe2Si phase... The dendritic cell boundaries form a continuous network approximately 14 nm in width. The α-Al cells have an average diameter of approximately 67 nm. The cell boundaries are amorphous (38%). The average grain size of the alloy is approximately 1.6 μm. Fine TiV particles, approximately 12 nm in size, are found embedded within the cells.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine equiaxed grains (~1.6 μm). Amorphous cell boundary network (~14 nm width). α-Al cells (~67 nm). Nanoscale TiV particles (~12 nm) and a minor fraction of Al8Fe2Si phase.
